svg.title: Hamster mini-alert v1 (scripted)
svg.desc: Mini-mode alert (notification) for clawd-on-desk / MiniCPM-Desk-Pet, right-edge variant (host mirrors for left edge). Storyboard confirmed by 鹿鹿 2026-06-12: golden exclamation pops up from below (0.45s easeOutBack overshoot) -> hamster snaps its head toward it (~150ms, step-fed look target into the verbatim mini-idle engine; feeding a continuous trajectory would be damped by the 150ms segment interpolator) -> after arriving: quick surprise-mouth open 0.16s, beat, quick close 0.18s while the mouth shrinks to the confirmed thinking small mouth (stroke-dasharray inset 7.5) and brows morph to the notification focus brows (BROW_POSES, kept for the rest of the state) -> turns back to the viewer as the exclamation fades out -> alarm-clock hold loop with zero pause: cyan-blue ripple arcs from both ears (#4fb8d8 on #2a7a96, every 1.7s), head sway ±3° at 4.4Hz about the neck (187,252) via the alert-head-sway wrapper, tuft float ±2.5° at 0.8Hz with offset phases, mouth-corner micro motion (inset 7.5±2.5, 4s). Engine drives breathing/look layers itself; alert writes only wrapper groups (alert-glasses-jump, alert-head-sway), per-tuft/per-brow nodes and screen-coordinate layers (ripples, exclamation), so the two never fight. Total entrance ≈2.2s then loops the hold forever; host auto-returns to idle after ~4s. Self-driven: needs no pointer feed; svg root keeps pointer-events:none so the engine's hover fallback can never steal the gaze in embedding pages. Exposes window.__miniAlert.replay() for demos. Pure paths + soft-lift filter, no image/gradient/fetch.
